dialogueChunks.intro: The deadline moved up, {player_name}. Three more people in {quest_focus} collapsed overnight. The council says they can't wait any longer.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found a partial remedy in the records but it requires {quest_item} from {location}. It won't cure everyone: maybe half, maybe fewer. The council is debating whether a partial cure justifies keeping the doors open. My sister is one of the sickest. She told me through the wall to let them seal it.
dialogueChunks.outro: I can't accept that. Get {quest_item} to {target_npc}. Even saving half is better than losing all of them. We're running out of time to find any answer.

playerResponses.text: Half is better than none. I'll get {quest_item}.
Your sister might be right. Sometimes love means letting go.

dialogueChunks.intro: The rescue plan hit a wall. The {enemy_type} moved the hostages to a second location deeper in their territory. {quest_ally} barely made it back alive.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} intercepted a message: the {enemy_type} are splitting the hostages into two groups held at different camps near {location}. We can reach one camp in time but not both. Four captives at one site, eight at the other. {quest_ally} says the smaller group includes the settlement's only healer.
dialogueChunks.outro: Choose which group to save and someone dies. Refuse to choose and everyone dies. Find {quest_item}: it might give us the edge to hit both camps, but it means going through hostile territory first.

playerResponses.text: I'll find a way to reach both. No one gets left behind.
Eight lives versus four. The numbers are clear.

dialogueChunks.intro: The bridge is weakening on its own, {player_name}. The weight of the crossing refugees is cracking the supports. We might not even need the torch.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found a shallow ford two miles downstream, but {enemy_type} scouts are already patrolling the bank there. {quest_ally} counted at least a dozen. The seventeen stranded people include three children and an elder who can barely walk. Getting them to the ford means moving slowly through open ground.
dialogueChunks.outro: Clear the ford of {enemy_type} patrols and mark the path with {quest_item}. If the bridge collapses before everyone crosses, the ford is their only chance. The commander is running out of reasons not to light the fuse.

playerResponses.text: I'll clear the ford. Get those people moving downstream.
Can the bridge hold long enough for them to cross normally?
Seventeen people against an army. You know what the math says.
